title: In focus: Improving the energy performance of buildings
content: This article takes a look at the many benefits of improving the energy performance of buildings and highlights the work that is already under way across the EU.
snippet: This article takes a look at the many benefits of improving the energy performance of buildings and highlights the work that is already under way across the EU.

title: Electric car sales surge as high oil prices drive shift away from fossil fuels
content: Electric car sales in Europe are accelerating as high oil prices make the switch away from fossil fuels increasingly attractive. With more affordable models, lower running costs and growing consumer demand, electric vehicles are becoming a mainstream choice across Europe.

title: Mediterranean Algae: restoring ports and coastal waters with BlueInvest
content: Mediterranean Algae, a Spanish company tackles nutrient pollution in ports and coastal waters. Its system uses algae to clean seawater. It also provides reliable real-time data on water quality. This helps ports meet EU requirements and reduce their environmental impact.

title: Europe's bathing waters remain safe to swim
content: The vast majority met the EU's ‘excellent' bathing quality standards in 2025, according to a new European Environment Agency report.
snippet: The vast majority met the EU's ‘excellent' bathing quality standards in 2025, according to a new European Environment Agency report.

title: New working group aims to create more housing for students and youth
content: Member States, universities and student associations joins force with the Commission to increase the amount of student and youth housing across Europe.

title: Commission launches two calls for evidence on the future ocean and water research and innovation strategy
content: Research and innovation news alert: Today, the European Commission has launched two calls for evidence to help shape the future Ocean and Water Research and Innovation (R&I) Strategy. This key follow-up initiative is expected to be adopted by the end of 2026.

title: Commissioner Roswall holds high-level dialogue on PFAS-related pollution challenges
content: Discussions in Brussels addressed the widespread pollution of 'forever chemicals' and strategies for their phase-out.
snippet: Discussions in Brussels addressed the widespread pollution of 'forever chemicals' and strategies for their phase-out.

title: Statement by VDL on the agreement between the United States and Iran
content: I welcome the agreement reached between the US and Iran, following sustained diplomatic efforts by several partners. The priority now is its swift and full implementation by all parties. This agreement should allow for the immediate reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.
snippet: I welcome the agreement reached between the US and Iran, following sustained diplomatic efforts by several partners. The priority now is its swift and full implementation by all parties. This

title: Commission approves €8 million Croatian State aid for fishing companies facing increased fuel prices
content: The European Commission has approved an €8 million Croatian State aid scheme to support fishing companies facing increased fuel prices due to the Middle East crisis. The scheme was approved under the Middle East Crisis Temporary State Aid Framework (METSAF) adopted by the Commission on 29 April 2026

title: Global Gateway: Council adopts conclusions on the EU’s global investment and partnership strategy
content: The Council today adopted conclusions on Global Gateway, reaffirming it as the European Union’s worldwide investment strategy for building mutually beneficial partnerships and mobilising public and private resources to bridge the global investment gap.

title: EU and Montenegro close accession negotiations on freedom of movement of workers and consumer and health protection
content: Today, the 27th meeting of the Accession Conference with Montenegro provisionally closed accession negotiations on chapters 2 (freedom of movement for workers) and 28 (consumer and health protection).
snippet: Today, the 27th meeting of the Accession Conference with Montenegro provisionally closed accession negotiations on chapters 2 (freedom of movement for workers) and 28 (consumer and health protection).

title: Commission welcomes landmark agreement on revised air passenger rights
content: The Commission welcomes the political agreement reached by the European Parliament and the Council of the EU on the revision of EU air passenger rights.
snippet: The Commission welcomes the political agreement reached by the European Parliament and the Council of the EU on the revision of EU air passenger rights.

title: EU and Ukraine open first accession negotiations cluster
content: Today, the EU and Ukraine reached a significant milestone by opening negotiations on the so-called 'fundamentals' cluster. This cluster covers the core values and principles on which the EU is built. It includes the EU acquis related to the rule of law and fundamental rights, the functioning of...

title: Seagrass power: how nature is healing a polluted lagoon
content: In Portugal’s Ria de Aveiro lagoon, the once-thriving Largo do Laranjo was a vibrant underwater meadow carpeted in Zostera noltei seagrass. But decades of industrial pollution left mercury and arsenic into the waters, poisoning the sediments and wiping out the underwater greenery.

title: EU Coordinators discussed the strengthened European Child Guarantee and support for vulnerable young people

title: Commission approves Holcim's acquisition of Xella, subject to conditions
content: The European Commission has approved, under the EU Merger Regulation, the proposed acquisition of Xella international S.A. ('Xella') by Holcim Ltd ('Holcim').

title: Statement by President von der Leyen and President Costa on the agreement to open the first accession negotiation cluster with Ukraine and Moldova
content: Today, the European Union took a major step forward. All Member States agreed to open the first accession negotiations cluster with Ukraine and Moldova. At the first Intergovernmental Conference on Monday, we will open the cluster on fundamentals; the backbone of the accession process.

title: Commission welcomes Council agreement on strengthening CBAM
content: The European Commission welcomes the Council agreement to extend the Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) to specific downstream goods and reinforce existing anti-circumvention safeguards.
snippet: The European Commission welcomes the Council agreement to extend the Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) to specific downstream goods and reinforce existing anti-circumvention safeguards.
